prompt_text: Retool vs Budibase pricing and features
response:
# Retool vs Budibase: Pricing & Features Comparison

## Pricing

| Feature | Retool | Budibase |
|---------|--------|----------|
| **Free Tier** | Yes (1 user, 5 apps) | Yes (unlimited apps, 1 user) |
| **Pro/Team** | $50-150/month per user | $50-150/month per user |
| **Enterprise** | Custom pricing | Custom pricing |
| **Self-hosting** | Available | Available (free) |
| **Pricing Model** | Per-user seat-based | Per-user + self-host free |

## Key Features Comparison

### Retool
✅ **Strengths:**
- 500+ pre-built connectors & integrations
- Superior drag-and-drop UI builder
- Real-time collaboration
- Better for complex enterprise apps
- Extensive documentation
- More mature ecosystem

❌ **Weaknesses:**
- Expensive at scale (per-user pricing)
- Steeper learning curve
- Less flexible self-hosting

### Budibase
✅ **Strengths:**
- Free self-hosting option
- More affordable cloud pricing
- Simpler interface for beginners
- Good for rapid prototyping
- Lower total cost of ownership
- Better for teams on budget

❌ **Weaknesses:**
- Fewer integrations (~50)
- Smaller community/ecosystem
- Less powerful automation
- Limited advanced features

## Quick Verdict

| Choose **Retool** if: | Choose **Budibase** if: |
|---|---|
| Building complex business apps | Building MVPs/simple tools |
| Need many integrations | Want to save money |
| Enterprise requirements | Prefer self-hosting |
| Team collaboration priority | Small/growing team |

**Bottom line:** Retool = more powerful but pricier; Budibase = budget-friendly and simpler.
